name: Janet M. Lane - Memphis TN - Bankruptcy Attorney at Law
description_short: Memphis-based bankruptcy attorney Janet M. Lane offers Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing services with 30+ years of experience helping Tennessee residents achieve debt relief.
description_long:
Janet M. Lane is a bankruptcy attorney based in Memphis, Tennessee, who has been practicing bankruptcy law for over 30 years. Her practice focuses exclusively on helping individuals and families navigate the bankruptcy process across multiple Tennessee communities, including Memphis, Germantown, Cordova, Arlington, Collierville, Millington, Covington, and surrounding areas. The firm emphasizes personalized service and client understanding throughout the bankruptcy filing experience.

The firm specializes in Chapter 7 bankruptcy (liquidation and unsecured debt elimination) and Chapter 13 bankruptcy (3-5 year repayment plans). Lane offers comprehensive debt relief services addressing wage garnishment, creditor harassment, judgments, tax issues, foreclosures, repossession, utility shutoffs, and medical debt. All consultations are offered at no charge, allowing clients to explore their options before committing to representation. The practice operates standard business hours (8am-5pm Monday through Friday) and provides multiple contact methods including phone, email, and website submission.

Lane distinguishes herself through extensive experience (30+ years in the field, with 15+ years of specialization specifically in bankruptcy), demonstrated client satisfaction through multiple positive reviews emphasizing her approachability and thoroughness, and a reputation for responsive communication even after cases conclude. Client testimonials highlight her ability to simplify complex processes and demonstrate both legal expertise and genuine care for client outcomes.

A realistic assessment: This is a solo bankruptcy attorney practice, not a high-volume bankruptcy mill. The website contains some inconsistencies in stated experience length (15 years vs. 30+ years of practice), which suggests potential marketing language that shouldn't be taken at face value. Prospective clients should verify Bar status and specific experience with their particular bankruptcy chapter. The practice appears legitimate and well-regarded, but bankruptcy cases require careful vetting of individual attorney credentials and fee structures, which are not detailed on the website.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 30+ years of bankruptcy practice experience in Tennessee
Free initial consultation to evaluate bankruptcy options
Specializes in both Chapter 7 (asset liquidation) and Chapter 13 (repayment plans)
Handles multiple related debt issues: wage garnishment, foreclosure, repossession, and creditor harassment
Multiple positive client reviews emphasizing responsiveness and client care
Established local practice serving Memphis and surrounding Tennessee communities
Available for ongoing advice and consultation even after case closure, per client testimonials
cons: Website contains inconsistent claims about years in practice (15 years vs. 30+ years), raising questions about marketing accuracy
No fee structure, payment plans, or cost information disclosed on website
Solo practitioner model may limit availability compared to larger bankruptcy firms
No specific information about Bar status, certifications, or disciplinary history provided on website
Limited detail about the bankruptcy process timeline, required documentation, or case outcomes
best_for: Memphis-area residents filing Chapter 7 bankruptcy to eliminate unsecured debts
Tennessee individuals facing wage garnishment, foreclosure, or creditor harassment needing legal intervention
Debtors seeking Chapter 13 repayment plan arrangements over 3-5 years
People seeking a local, established bankruptcy attorney with strong client testimonials
diagnosis: Janet M. Lane is best for Memphis-area and Tennessee residents who need a local bankruptcy attorney for straightforward Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filings and want a responsive, experienced solo practitioner. The main caveat is verifying her current Bar standing and understanding fee structures before engaging, as these critical details are absent from her website—and prospective clients should reconcile the conflicting experience claims before assuming 30+ years of bankruptcy specialization.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing (3-5 year repayment plans)
Creditor harassment litigation and cease-and-desist representation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
